name: Glittery Gift Cake
recipeIngredient: 6 oz Vanilla milk chips or vanilla candy coating
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 13x9-inch pan and line it with waxed paper, then grease again.
2. Prepare the cake according to the recipe directions or pack instructions.
3. Cool slightly, then carefully remove the cake from the pan.
4. Remove the waxed paper from the cake and cool completely.
5. Invert the cake onto a large tray or foil-covered cardboard.
6. Frost the sides and top of the cake.
7. To prepare ribbons and bow:
a. In a small saucepan, combine vanilla chips and corn syrup.
b. Melt over low heat, stirring constantly until smooth.
c. Refrigerate for 15-20 minutes or until firm enough to knead.
d. Knead until smooth.
e. Divide the mixture into two parts (1/4 and 3/4).
f. Add a few drops of green food coloring to the 1/4 portion and wrap in plastic wrap.
g. Add a few drops of red food coloring to the 3/4 portion and place between two sheets of parchment paper or on a surface sprinkled lightly with powdered sugar.
h. Roll out the mixture into a 16x6-inch rectangle.
i. Cut lengthwise into 3/4-inch strips using a knife or pastry wheel.
j. Place the strips on the cake to resemble ribbon, folding additional strips to form a bow.
k. Roll out the green mixture to about an eighth inch thickness.
l. Using a holly leaf cutter, cut out eight leaves.
m. Score veins with the tip of a knife.
n. Tuck the leaves around the bow for garnish.
o. Sprinkle the ribbon and leaves with edible glitter.
TIPS: Edible glitter is available at kitchen specialty shops. To prevent parchment paper from slipping while rolling out ribbons, tape it to the counter.
